title: The Effects of Generative AI on High-Skilled Work: Evidence from Three Field Experiments with Software Developers

metadata.title: The Effects of Generative AI on High-Skilled Work: Evidence from Three Field Experiments with Software Developers
metadata.abstract: We conduct three randomized controlled trials with software developers at Microsoft, Accenture, and a Fortune 100 company to examine the causal impact of generative AI coding tools on developer productivity, code quality, and job satisfaction.
key_findings: 26% productivity increase for developers using GitHub Copilot
Larger gains for less experienced developers
Productivity effects vary by developer experience level
Sustained effects over 6+ months of use
metadata.methodology_details: RCT design, random assignment to treatment/control, blinded evaluation
metadata.external_validity: Multiple firms, diverse developer experience levels

title: Measuring the Impact of Early-2025 AI on Experienced Open-Source Developer Productivity

metadata.title: Measuring the Impact of Early-2025 AI on Experienced Open-Source Developer Productivity
metadata.methodology: RCT, field experiment with experienced developers
metadata.abstract: Randomized controlled trial examining how AI tools at the frontier (Feb-June 2025) affect the productivity of experienced open-source developers on their own repositories.
key_findings: 19% increase in task completion time with AI enabled
AI makes experienced developers slower on exploration tasks
Different task types show heterogeneous treatment effects
Experience level is critical moderator of AI effectiveness
metadata.methodology_details: RCT with random assignment, within-subject crossover design

title: The Impact of AI-Powered Code Assistance on Developer Productivity: Evidence from Google

metadata.title: The Impact of AI-Powered Code Assistance on Developer Productivity: Evidence from Google
metadata.abstract: Randomized controlled trial at Google examining how AI-powered code assistance affects developer productivity and time spent on tasks.
key_findings: 21% reduction in time spent on tasks
Larger effects for routine tasks vs complex tasks
Code quality maintained despite speed improvements
High adoption and satisfaction among participants
metadata.methodology_details: RCT with 96 full-time engineers, logged task times

metadata.title: Generative AI at Work
metadata.methodology: RCT, field experiment in customer support
metadata.abstract: Randomized controlled trial examining how access to generative AI suggestions affects productivity of customer support agents.
key_findings: 14% productivity increase for agents with AI access
Larger gains for less experienced agents (35% improvement)
Improved job satisfaction
Quality metrics maintained or improved
metadata.methodology_details: RCT at single firm, random assignment of AI access

title: Dear Diary: A Randomized Controlled Trial of Generative AI Coding Tools in the Workplace

metadata.title: Dear Diary: A Randomized Controlled Trial of Generative AI Coding Tools in the Workplace
metadata.abstract: Workplace randomized controlled trial of generative AI coding tools with crossed design to measure causal effects on various productivity dimensions.
key_findings: 50% time savings in code documentation
Heterogeneous effects across task types
Learning curves evident over study period
metadata.methodology_details: RCT with within-subject design, task-level measurement

title: Impact: Randomized Controlled Trial of AI Evaluation Methods

metadata.title: Impact: Randomized Controlled Trial of AI Evaluation Methods
metadata.abstract: Randomized controlled trial examining best practices for evaluating AI impact on human performance and productivity.
key_findings: Identification of key confounders in AI evaluation
Importance of task-specific outcome measurement
Learning effects require longer study periods

title: The Estimation of Causal Effects by Difference-in-Differences

metadata.title: The Estimation of Causal Effects by Difference-in-Differences
metadata.abstract: Foundational paper on difference-in-differences methodology for causal inference with quasi-experimental data.
key_findings: DID removes time-invariant unobserved heterogeneity
Parallel trends assumption is critical
Extensions to multiple treatments and time periods
metadata.methodology_details: Theoretical and practical guidance on DID estimation
metadata.external_validity: General methodology applicable to many settings
